body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.Translation.Document` **Service directory:** `sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/` **Triggered by:** PR #56970 (commit 085c6ca) by @JonathanCrd ## What Changed PR #56970 migrated `Azure.AI.Translation.Document` to the new Azure Data Plane SDK generator. The changes include: - Migrated codegen to use the new TypeSpec-based generator (updated `tsp-location.yaml` and project file) - Renamed `DocumentTranslationModelFactory` internal structure (Model Factory rename) - Custom code for `StartTranslation` removed ‚Äî now fully generated - Credential logic in the custom layer updated after regeneration - `Translate` and `TranslateAsync` methods made public in the custom layer - `ForwardsClientCalls` attribute removed from client methods - `ExperimentalAttribute` added to project compilation sources - Diagnostic scopes fixed in Pageable methods ## Gaps Found - **CHANGELOG `2.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` is completely empty** ‚Äî all subsections ("Features Added", "Breaking Changes", "Bugs Fixed", "Other Changes") are blank despite significant changes landing in this version - **README does not mention `ExperimentalAttribute`** ‚Äî the library now marks itself as experimental but there is no note for users in the README about this status or what it implies - **README "Authenticate the client" section still only shows `AzureKeyCredential` and `DefaultAzureCredential`** ‚Äî the updated credential logic in the custom layer (post-regeneration) is not reflected with a note about any behavioral changes - **No mention of `SingleDocumentTranslationClient.Translate` / `TranslateAsync` visibility change** ‚Äî these methods are now public (custom layer) but this change is not surfaced in docs or changelog
üìê Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/CHANGELOG.md` Edit the `## 2.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section. Replace the empty body with entries reflecting the actual changes from PR #56970: ```markdown ## 2.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased) ### Features Added - `SingleDocumentTranslationClient.Translate` and `SingleDocumentTranslationClient.TranslateAsync` are now publicly accessible. ### Breaking Changes - The library is now generated using the new Azure TypeSpec-based SDK generator. Some internal implementation details have changed, though the public API surface remains largely compatible with 2.0.0. - `ForwardsClientCalls` attribute has been removed from client methods. ### Other Changes - Migrated code generation to the new TypeSpec-based Azure SDK generator for improved maintainability. - Updated credential handling logic in the client custom layer. - `ExperimentalAttribute` has been added to the package; this library is under active development and the API surface may change. ``` #### File 2: `sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/README.md` Add a note at the top of the **Getting started** section (right after the introductory paragraph and the badge links, before the `## Getting started` heading) to inform users of the experimental status: ```markdown > **Note:** This library is currently in an experimental state. The API surface may change in future releases. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new snippet test files need to be created for these changes ‚Äî the existing samples in `sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/tests/Samples/` cover the main usage patterns. However, if the agent wants to add a snippet showing `SingleDocumentTranslationClient.Translate` usage, follow this structure: 1. **Write sample code** in `sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/tests/Samples/Sample6_SingleDocumentTranslation.cs` (check if it already exists; if so, update it): - Wrap the `Translate` method example with `#region Snippet:SingleDocumentTranslation` / `#endregion` - Add `[Test]` attribute 2. **Add placeholder in README** in the `## Examples` section under a new heading `### Translate a single document`: ``` ```C# Snippet:SingleDocumentTranslation ``` ``` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README currently contains all required sections. Verify each is complete: 1. ‚úÖ Getting started (install, prerequisites, authenticate) 2. ‚úÖ Key concepts 3. ‚úÖ Examples (async and sync) 4. ‚úÖ Troubleshooting (via reference links) 5. ‚úÖ Next steps 6. ‚úÖ Contributing The main gap is the missing experimental status note and the incomplete CHANGELOG. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 translation` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 translation` 5. `dotnet format sdk/translation/Azure.AI.Translation.Document/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [Translation] Docs: Update CHANGELOG and README after generator migration
